title: Trump Witnesses Return of Bodies of 6 U.S. Service Members
summary: President Trump traveled to Dover Air Force Base in Delaware to witness the transfer of the remains of five men and one woman killed in an Iranian drone strike.

title: In War’s First Week, a Punishing Military Campaign With No Coherent Endgame
summary: The U.S. and Israel have pounded Iran’s leadership and undercut its defense capabilities, but President Trump has offered wildly different explanations for what he hopes to achieve.

title: How We Analyzed the Strike on the Iranian School
summary: Malachy Browne of our Visual Investigations team describes what satellite imagery and other evidence tell us about who might be responsible for an airstrike on an elementary school in southern Iran. The strike killed at least 175 people, according to health officials and Iranian state media.

title: How D.H.S. Retreated on Immigration Tactics After Minneapolis
summary: Agents have conducted more targeted operations, rather than street sweeps, leading to a dip in arrests — at least for now.

title: Judge Voids Mass Layoffs at Voice of America
summary: The ruling, which said that Kari Lake’s appointment to oversee V.O.A.’s parent agency was invalid, was a major rejection of President Trump’s attempts to dismantle the government-funded news group.

title: When DOGE Unleashed ChatGPT on the Humanities
summary: Documents show how A.I. was used to cancel most previously approved grants by the National Endowment for the Humanities as the agency embraced President Trump’s agenda.

title: Trump Assembles a New Coalition to ‘Eradicate’ Cartels
summary: At a gathering in Florida, the president asked the leaders of a dozen Latin American and Caribbean nations to help the U.S. military crush armed trafficking groups.

title: At Noma, Accusations of Past Physical Abuse
summary: Dozens of former employees say René Redzepi inflicted physical and psychological violence on the staff for years.

title: How Alberto Carvalho Became L.A.U.S.D. Superintendent Despite Scandals
summary: Alberto Carvalho was seen as a catch for the nation’s second-largest school district. Then his home and office were raided by the F.B.I.

title: In Russia’s Former Capital, Restoring the Past to Survive the Present
summary: With political activism off-limits, residents of St. Petersburg are finding purpose and community in the “politics of small deeds,” repairing and cleaning architectural treasures.

title: The Badlands Hold Me as I Grieve
summary: I imagine the birds I see are the family members I’ve lost.

title: Smoking Jars of Metal and Fuses Thrown at Protest Near Mayor’s House
summary: Six people were arrested after anti-Islamic protesters led by the right-wing activist Jake Lang clashed with counterprotesters near Gracie Mansion.

title: Gen Z-Backed Rapper Is on Course to Lead Nepal With Landslide Win
summary: Balendra Shah, the onetime rap artist and former mayor of the country’s capital, is on course to become the country’s next prime minister.

title: Epstein Doctor Steps Away From Elite Health Clinics
summary: Dr. Bernard Kruger once gave a private E.R. membership to Jeffrey Epstein and unnamed “girls.”

title: Young New Yorkers Decided the Revolution Will Have Roommates
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s focus on freezing the rent as part of his affordability platform has unleashed a new political force of younger renters.
